name: 抛体运动
subject: physics
curriculum_points: 通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。
体会将复杂运动分解为简单运动的物理思想。能分析生产生活中的抛体运动。

deep_snippets.text:
Figure 5.29 (a) We analyze two-dimensional projectile motion by breaking it into two independent one-dimensional motions along the vertical and horizontal axes. (b) The horizontal motion is simple, because and is thus constant. (c) The velocity in the vertical direction begins to decrease as the object rises; at its highest point, the vertical velocity is zero. As the object falls towards the Earth again, the vertical velocity increases again in magnitude but points in the opposite direction to the initial vertical velocity. (d) The x- and y-motions are recombined to give the total velocity at any given point on the trajectory.
For problems of projectile motion, it is important to set up a coordinate system. The first step is to choose an initial position for and . Usually, it is simplest to set the initial position of the object so that and . 5.3 • Projectile Motion

Call the maximum height ; then, This equation defines the maximum height of a projectile. The maximum height depends only on the vertical component of the initial velocity.
Calculating Projectile Motion: Hot Rock Projectile Suppose a large rock is ejected from a volcano, as illustrated in Figure 5.31, with a speed of and at an angle above the horizontal. The rock strikes the side of the volcano at an altitude 20.0 m lower than its starting point. (a) Calculate the time it takes the rock to follow this path. Figure 5.31 The diagram shows the projectile motion of a large rock from a volcano. Strategy Breaking this two-dimensional motion into two independent one-dimensional motions will allow us to solve for the time. The time a projectile is in the air depends only on its vertical motion. Solution While the rock is in the air, it rises and then falls to a final position 20.0 m lower than its starting altitude. We can find the time for this by using If we take the initial position to be zero, then the final position is Now the initial vertical velocity is the vertical component of the initial velocity, found from Substituting known values yields Rearranging terms gives a quadratic equation in This expression is a quadratic equation of the form , where the constants are a = 4.90, b = –14.3, and c = –20.0. Its solutions are given by the quadratic formula 5.9
> **[Figure 125, Page 182]** A figure or diagram

Check Your Understanding 13. What is projectile motion? a. Projectile motion is the motion of an object projected into the air, which moves under the influence of gravity. b. Projectile motion is the motion of an object projected into the air which moves independently of gravity. c. Projectile motion is the motion of an object projected vertically upward into the air which moves under the influence of gravity. d. Projectile motion is the motion of an object projected horizontally into the air which moves independently of gravity. 14. What is the force experienced by a projectile after the initial force that launched it into the air in the absence of air resistance? a. The nuclear force b. The gravitational force c. The electromagnetic force d. The contact force 5.4 Inclined Planes Section Learning Objectives By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Distinguish between static friction and kinetic friction • Solve problems involving inclined planes Section Key Terms kinetic friction static friction Static Friction and Kinetic Friction Recall from the previous chapter that friction is a force that opposes motion, and is around us all the time. Friction allows us to move, which you have discovered if you have ever tried to walk on ice. There are different types of friction—kinetic and static. Kinetic friction acts on an object in motion, while static friction acts on an object or system at rest. The maximum static friction is usually greater than the kinetic friction between the objects. supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 抛体运动

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 10
- **章节/领域**: 相互作用与牛顿定律
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。
> 体会将复杂运动分解为简单运动的物理思想。能分析生产生活中的抛体运动。

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「抛体运动」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「抛体运动」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「抛体运动」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「抛体运动」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「抛体运动」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 围绕课标要点“通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「抛体运动」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系工程、能源、交通或日常生活中的相关现象，讨论「抛体运动」的应用边界。
- 补充科学史或技术应用案例，帮助学生理解物理规律如何服务真实问题。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
抛体运动是物理学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以可观察实验或生活现象引入「抛体运动」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 探究建构：强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

混淆现象描述与物理规律

exercises.stem: 依据课标「抛体运动」：通过实验，探究并认识平抛运动的规律。会用运动合成与分解的方法分析平抛运动。 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：体会将复杂运动分解为简单运动的物理思想。能分析生产生活中的抛体运动。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「抛体运动」相关的物理规律。
